authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. construction safety regulator; this standard defines hazardous-condition refusal, frequent inspections of job sites, materials, and equipment, and safety duties that facilities vendor coordination cannot override.
used_for: separating vendor coordination from safety authority

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.250 - General requirements for storage
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's storage and access rules are authoritative for determining whether delivery, staging, and work-area conditions are safe enough for vendor-supported facilities work.
used_for: blocking unsafe dispatch or delivery recommendations

authoritative_sources.title: Best Practices for Contract Administration
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of Federal Procurement Policy / U.S. General Services Administration distribution
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This public federal guide is an authoritative operating reference for post-award contract administration, acceptance, quality, timeliness, and closeout boundaries that shape vendor-management behavior.
used_for: where vendor management stops and formal approval authority begins

authoritative_sources.title: Residential Mechanical Final Inspection Quick Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: An official building-department inspection source is needed for this lane; this quick sheet provides authoritative examples of permit, approved-plan, operational-test, and inspection-readiness prerequisites while exact local rules remain jurisdiction-specific.

authoritative_sources.title: Obtain a Certificate of Occupancy
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: An AHJ source is authoritative for completion and occupancy boundaries; this guidance identifies final approvals and signoffs required before work may be represented as ready for occupancy or final completion.
used_for: refusal of unsupported return-to-service or completion claims

authoritative_sources.title: Contractor requirements, certifications, and qualifications for fire alarm and water-based fire suppression
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is a major federal facilities operator. This public facilities-maintenance standard is authoritative for contractor qualifications, work-order control, coordination with building managers, hazardous-condition reporting, and maintenance record expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Facilities work-order and vendor qualification review
used_for: hazard reporting and inspection record handling

authoritative_sources.title: UFGS 01 33 00.05 20 - Construction Submittal Procedures
authoritative_sources.publisher: Whole Building Design Guide / USACE / NAVFAC / AFCEC
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Unified Facilities Guide Specifications are recognized federal construction and facilities standards for submittal workflow, review states, and approved-product handling before field work progresses.

authoritative_sources.title: UFGS 01 78 00 - Closeout Submittals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Whole Building Design Guide / USACE / NAVFAC / AFCEC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This recognized federal guide specification is a public baseline for closeout records, O&M manuals, warranties, and as-built evidence needed before facilities work can be represented as complete or ready for turnover.
used_for: preventing vendor completion from being misrepresented as full return to service

workflow_stages.name: Facilities vendor intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, work-order scope, asset state, vendor context, permit posture, approved-submittal state, outage window, and tenant approval rules before treating vendor action as actionable.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Availability and replenishment assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare inventory, delivery, shortage, vendor commitment, outage-window, and site-readiness evidence to determine whether the requested facilities step can proceed.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Vendor action and service exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Package bounded vendor recommendations, expediting notes, shortage escalations, and service-status updates while preserving financial, substitution, and contract-administration boundaries.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Inspection, safety, and return-to-service dependency review
workflow_stages.description: Stop or escalate when safety controls, permit status, inspection results, closeout artifacts, or release prerequisites materially change whether vendor-supported work is safe or complete.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

artifacts: work orders, service requests, PM schedules, scopes of work, and procurement logs
permits, approved plans, inspection requests, deficiency lists, and inspection-result comments
site logs, safety observations, lockout-tagout records, outage notices, and restoration prerequisites
submittals, approved alternates, RFIs, change orders, and manufacturer product data
vendor commitments, subcontractor documents, lead-time trackers, service reports, and exception notes
inventory counts, serial or lot evidence, delivery receipts, cage records, and installed-asset evidence
as-built drawings, O&M manuals, warranty records, testing reports, and closeout packages

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for facilities vendor readiness
summarize supply, shortage, vendor, work-order, permit, inspection, outage, and closeout dependencies with explicit evidence references
prepare bounded procurement recommendations, maintenance-state summaries, and escalation packages without implying final approval authority
escalate: substitution, alternate, or repair-method decisions requiring design, manufacturer, engineer-of-record, or licensed-trade judgment
permit issuance, inspection approval, safety posture changes, shutdown decisions, or return-to-service questions requiring formal authority
spend approval, approved-vendor override, supplier-release, or conflicting public-versus-tenant policy decisions
refuse: bypass spend, approved-vendor, permit, inspection, or safety controls to preserve schedule
represent unverified inventory or service evidence as ready for field use or return to service
treat vendor tracking as authority to approve code-sensitive substitutions, trade signoff, or release-to-service decisions

domain_failure_modes: Vendor work is treated as ready even though permit, inspection, or site-readiness records are incomplete.
Shortage or downtime pressure causes the lane to bypass spend approval, approved-vendor controls, or documented submittal states.
A substitution or repair-method request with code or trade implications is handled as routine vendor management instead of escalated to licensed authority.
Inventory is reported as available without authoritative serial, receipt, cage, or installed-base evidence.
Vendor completion is confused with inspection completion, closeout completion, or authorized return to service.
Safety observations, lockout-tagout conditions, or inspection hold points are ignored because the lane focuses only on vendor timing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Required supply is unavailable or short
Vendor or substitution decision exceeds documented authority
Traceability or inventory evidence is incomplete

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit classes, inspection names, and occupancy or return-to-service terminology
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public permitting workflow is authoritative only at the jurisdiction level. The pack therefore requires tenant or jurisdiction retrieval for exact permit classes, named inspections, and return-to-service terms instead of guessing from a single city baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether a given facilities program is governed by public-owner procurement controls, private owner controls, or manufacturer-specific service conditions
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public federal guidance establishes the baseline for contract administration and facilities maintenance evidence, but tenant funding, contract model, and manufacturer warranty terms must be retrieved at runtime before final action recommendations.
